claim: Larger language models are not automatically safer or better at following instructions; they can understand jailbreaks encoded in poems better than small models, creating more attack surface
actionable: When evaluating model selection for agents, consider hidden costs of larger models including vulnerability to sophisticated jailbreaks and misuse of broad capabilities
source_title: Spec-Driven Testing for Agents With A Brain the Size of A Planet — Steven Willmott, SafeIntelligence

claim: Agent specs should include rules, domain ontologies, rights/roles, and robustness requirements independent of implementation to enable model swaps and security testing
actionable: Define explicit agent specifications with rules, domain ontologies, and robustness requirements before development to enable testing across model changes
source_title: Spec-Driven Testing for Agents With A Brain the Size of A Planet — Steven Willmott, SafeIntelligence

claim: Robustness testing for agents should validate behavior under stress: typos, rephrasings, and domain-specific substitutions (e.g., gross profit vs gross sales)
actionable: Build test suites that measure agent stability across typos, rephrasings, and domain-specific terminology variations
source_title: Spec-Driven Testing for Agents With A Brain the Size of A Planet — Steven Willmott, SafeIntelligence
